mission.title: crossplane: Composition Dependency and Ordered Creation
mission.description: Composition Dependency and Ordered Creation. Requested by 11+ users.

steps.title: Check current crossplane deployment
steps.description:
Verify your crossplane version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n crossplane -l app.kubernetes.io/name=crossplane
helm list -n crossplane 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working crossplane installation.

steps.title: Review crossplane configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant crossplane configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n crossplane -l app.kubernetes.io/name=crossplane
kubectl get configmap -n crossplane -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=crossplane
```
### What problem are you facing?
Although my default Crossplane creates all resources in a composition in order, this typically does not include the entire reconciliation lifecycle for provisioning some abstract resource.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Composition Dependency and Ordered Creation
steps.description:
Hmm, interesting, could #1770 be used to create a reference from a job to some other resource such that the creation is blocked until the other resource finishes being provisioned? I could see that working, although it does feel like a slightly hacky solution. 

As for what I mentioned before,
```yaml
Name:         apqa-test-1-g7nff
Namespace:    
Labels:       crossplane.io/claim-name=
              crossplane.io/claim-namespace=
              crossplane.io/composite=apqa-test-1
              name=eks-cluster-role
Annotations:  crossplane.io/composition-resource-name: eks-cluster-role
              crossplane.io/external-create-pending: 2022-02-01T17:43:13Z
              crossplane.io/external-create-succeeded: 2022-02-01T17:43:14Z
              crossplane.io/external-name: apqa-test-1-g7nff
API Version:  iam.aws.crossplane.io/v1beta1
Kind:         Role
Metadata:
  Creation Timestamp:  202
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n crossplane -l app.kubernetes.io/name=crossplane
kubectl get events -n crossplane --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Composition Dependency and Ordered Creation" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: Hmm, interesting, could #1770 be used to create a reference from a job to some other resource such that the creation is blocked until the other resource finishes being provisioned? I could see that working, although it does feel like a slightly hacky solution.

            roleArnSelector:
              matchControllerRef: true
              matchLabels:
                name: eks-cluster-role

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with crossplane installed or the issue environment reproducible.
